title: Linear equations word problems
description: Solve word problems about real world relationships that are given in formulas.  Functions are written using function notation.

assessment_items.question_content:
A charity organization is having a fundraiser.

$P$ represents the fundraiser's profit (in dollars) if $n$ tickets are sold. A negative profit means the expenses exceeded the income from tickets.

$P=70n-1500$

**What are the expenses of the fundraiser?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: Before any tickets are sold, all we have is the expenses. So the fundraiser's expenses are equal to the value of $-P$ when $n=0$.

$\begin{align}
P&=70n-1500\\\\
&=70(0)-1500\\\\
&=-1500
\end{align}$

The expenses were $1500$ more than the income before they sold any tickets.
The expenses of the fundraiser are $\$1500$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rachel is a stunt driver, and she's escaping from a building that is about to explode!

$D$ represents Rachel's remaining distance (in meters) after $t$ seconds.

$D=-38t+220$

**How long is the distance Rachel has to drive?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] meters
hints: Before any time has passed, Rachel's remaining distance is the entire distance she has to drive. So the entire distance is equal to the value of $D$ when $t=0$.
The entire distance Rachel has to drive is $220$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Harry took a loan from the bank.

$D$ represents Harry's remaining debt (in dollars) after $t$ months.

$D=-200t+9000$

**What was the size of Harry's loan?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: When Harry hadn't paid any of his debt, his debt was the size of his loan. So the size of Harry's loan is equal to the value of $D$ when $t=0$.
The size of Harry's loan is $\$9000$.

assessment_items.question_content:
When Quinn got home, he turned the air conditioner on.

$Q$ represents the temperature in Quinn's home (in degrees Celsius) after $t$ minutes.

$Q=42-0.7t$

**What was the temperature when Quinn returned home?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] degrees Celsius
hints: When Quinn returned home, it was $0$ minutes since he turned on the air conditioner. So the temperature at that time is equal to the value of $Q$ when $t=0$.
When Quinn returned home, the temperature was $42$ degrees Celsius.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mr. Mole left his burrow and started digging his way down.

$A$ represents Mr. Mole's altitude relative to the ground (in meters) after $t$ minutes.

$A=-2.3t-7$

**How far below the ground does Mr. Mole's burrow lie?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] meters below the ground
hints: Before any time has passed, Mr. Mole was in his burrow. So the burrow's altitude is equal to the value of $A$ when $t=0$.

$\begin{align} 
A &=-2.3t-7\\\\
&=-2.3(0)-7\\\\
&=-7
\end{align}$

A negative value represents a distance below the ground.
Mr. Mole's burrows lies $7$ meters below the ground.

answers.message: The burrow is not a negative distance below the ground.

assessment_items.question_content:
Andrei wants to fill a glass tank with marbles, and then fill the remaining space with water.

$W$ represents the volume of water Andrei uses (in liters) if he uses $n$ marbles.

$W=32-0.05n$

**What is the glass tank's volume?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] liters
hints: If Andrei doesn't use any marbles, he has to fill the entire tank with water. So the tank's volume is equal to the value of $W$ when $n=0$.
The glass tank's volume is $32$ liters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Agent Hunt is transferring classified files from the CIA mainframe into his flash drive.

$S$ represents the size of the files on the drive (in megabytes) after $t$ seconds.

$S=5t+45$

**What was the file size on the drive before the transfer?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] megabytes
hints: The file size before the transfer is the same as the file size at the moment when the transfer began. This is equal to the value of $S$ when $t=0$.
The file size when the transfer began was $45$ megabytes.

assessment_items.question_content:
A lake near the Arctic Circle is covered by sheet of ice during the cold winter months. When spring arrives, the ice starts to melt.

$S$ models the ice sheet's thickness (in meters) after $t$ weeks.

$S=-0.25t+4$

**What is the ice sheet's thickness at the beginning of spring?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints: The ice sheet's thickness at the beginning of spring is equal to the value of $S$ when $t=0$.
The ice sheet's thickness at the beginning of spring is $4$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A young sumo wrestler decided to go on a special diet to gain weight rapidly.

$W$ represents the wrestler's weight (in kilograms) after $t$ months.

$W=80+5.4t$

**What was the wrestler's weight before his special diet?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] kilograms
hints: The wrestler's weight before his special diet is equal to the value of $W$ when $t=0$.
The wrestler's weight before his special diet is $80$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
Temperature can be measured in two different common units: degrees Celsius and degrees Fahrenheit.

$f$ represents the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit as a function of the temperature $c$ in degrees Celsius.

$f=32+1.8c$

**Water freezes at $0$ degrees Celsius. What is the freezing temperature of water in degrees Fahrenheit?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] degrees Fahrenheit
hints: The freezing temperature of water in degrees Fahrenheit is equal to the value of $f$ when $c=0$.
Water's freezing temperature is $32$ degrees Fahrenheit.

assessment_items.question_content:
At Simba Travel Agency, the price of a climbing trip to Mount Kilimanjaro includes an initial fee plus a constant fee per meter.

$F$ represents the fee (in dollars) for climbing $d$ meters.

$F=110+0.12d$

**What is the increase in price for every $100$ meters climbed?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The increase in price for $100$ meters is $100$ times the unit rate of the price function.
The equation for $F$ is in *slope-intercept form*, so the slope of the graph is $0.12$. This means the unit rate is $0.12$ dollars per meter.
Therefore, for $100$ meters, the increase in price is $100\cdot \$0.12=\$12$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A big ship drops its anchor.

$E$ represents the anchor's elevation relative to the water's surface (in meters) as a function of time $t$ (in seconds).

$E=-2.4t+75$

**How far does the anchor drop every $5$ seconds?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints: The drop for $5$ seconds is $5$ times the unit drop rate of the function.
The equation for $E$ is in *slope-intercept form*, so the slope of the graph is $-2.4$. This means the unit drop rate is $2.4$ meters per second.
Therefore, for $5$ seconds, the anchor drops $5\cdot 2.4=12$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A young sumo wrestler decided to go on a special diet to gain weight rapidly.

$W$ represents the wrestler's weight (in kilograms) as a function of time $t$ (in months).

$W=80+5.4t$

**How much weight does the wrestler gain every $2$ months?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilograms
hints: The increase in weight for $2$ months is $2$ times the unit rate of the weight function.
The equation for $W$ is in *slope-intercept form*, so the slope of the graph is $5.4$. This means the unit rate is $5.4$ kilograms per month.
Therefore, for $2$ months, the weight gain is $2\cdot 5.4=10.8$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
Temperature can be measured in two different common units: degrees Celsius and degrees Fahrenheit.

$f$ represents the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit as a function of the temperature $c$ in degrees Celsius.

$f=32+1.8c$

**What is the temperature increase in degrees Fahrenheit that is equivalent to a temperature increase by $10$ degrees Celsius?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] degrees Fahrenheit
hints: The increase in temperature by $10$ degrees Celsius is $10$ times the unit rate of the conversion function.
The equation for $f$ is in *slope-intercept form*, so the slope of the graph is $1.8$. This means the unit rate is $1.8$ degrees Fahrenheit per $1$ degree Celsius.
Therefore, for $10$ degrees Celsius, the increase in Fahrenheit is $10\cdot 1.8=18$ degrees Fahrenheit.

assessment_items.question_content:
Agent Hunt is transferring classified files from the CIA mainframe into his flash drive.

$S$ represents the size of the files on the drive (in megabytes) after $t$ seconds.

$S=5t+45$

**How many megabytes are transferred every $10$ seconds?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] megabytes
hints: The size of the files being transferred every $10$ seconds is $10$ times the unit rate of the transfer.
The equation for $S$ is in *slope-intercept form*, so the slope of the graph is $5$. This means the unit rate is $5$ megabytes per second.
Therefore, in $10$ seconds, $10\cdot5=50$ megabytes are transferred.

assessment_items.question_content:
A paintball court charges an initial entrance fee plus a fixed price per ball.

$P$ represents the total price (in dollars) as a function of the number of balls used $n$.

$P=0.80n+5.50$

**What is the price for $10$ balls, not including the entrance fee?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The price of each ball is the relationship's rate of change, which in linear relationships is represented by the slope of the graph.
The equation for $P$ is in *slope-intercept form*, so the slope of the graph is $0.80$. This means that each ball costs $\$0.80$.
Therefore, the price of $10$ balls is $10\cdot \$0.80=\$8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Anumeha mows lawns. She charges an initial fee and a constant fee for each hour of work.

$F$ represents Anumeha's fee (in dollars) for working $t$ hours.

$F=6+12t$

**How much does Anumeha charge for each hour of work?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: The amount Anumeha charges for each hour of work is the relationship's rate of change, which in linear relationships is represented by the slope of the graph.
The equation for $F$ is in *slope-intercept form*. This means that the slope of the graph is $12$.
Therefore, Anumeha charges  $\$12$ for each hour.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ever since Renata moved to her new home, she's been keeping track of the height of the tree outside her window.

$H$ represents the height of the tree (in centimeters), $t$ years since Renata moved in.

$H=210+33t$

**How fast does the tree grow?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters per year
hints: The speed of the tree's growth is the relationship's rate of change, which in linear relationships is represented by the slope of the graph.
The equation for $H$ is in *slope-intercept form*. This means that the slope of the graph is $33$.
Therefore, the tree grows by $33$ centimeters per year.

assessment_items.question_content:
When Quinn got home, he turned the air conditioner on.

$T$ represents the temperature in Quinn's home (in degrees Celsius) after $t$ minutes.

$T=42-0.7t$

**How fast did the temperature drop?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] degrees Celsius per minute
hints: The temperature change per minute is the relationship's rate of change, which in linear relationships is represented by the slope of the graph.

The equation for $T$ is in *slope-intercept form*. This means that the slope of the graph is $-0.7$.

A slope of $-0.7$ means that the room's temperature drops by $0.7^\circ$ Celsius each minute.
The temperature dropped by $0.7$ degrees Celsius per minute.

answers.message: We already said the temperature dropped, so it shouldn't drop by a negative amount.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mr. Mole left his burrow and started digging his way down.

$A$ represents Mr. Mole's altitude relative to the ground (in meters) after $t$ minutes.

$A=-2.3t-7$

**How fast did Mr. Mole descend?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters per minute
hints: Mr. Mole's descent speed is the relationship's rate of change, which in linear relationships is represented by the slope of the graph.

The equation for $A$ is in *slope-intercept form*. This means that the slope of the graph is $-2.3$.

A slope of $-2.3$ means that Mr. Mole is descending by $2.3$ meters each minute.
Mr. Mole descended at $2.3$ meters per minute.

assessment_items.question_content:
Andrei wants to fill a glass tank with marbles, and then fill the remaining space with water.

$W$ represents the volume of water Andrei uses (in liters) if he uses $n$ marbles.

$W=32-0.05n$

**What is the volume of each marble?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] liters
hints: The volume of each marble is the relationship's rate of change, which in linear relationships is represented by the slope of the graph.

The equation for $W$ is in *slope-intercept form*. This means that the slope of the graph is $-0.05$.

A slope of $-0.05$ means that each marble reduces the needed amount of water by $0.05$ liters.
The volume of each marble is $0.05$ liters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rachel is a stunt driver, and she's escaping from a building that is about to explode!

$D$ represents Rachel's remaining distance (in meters) as a function of time $t$ (in seconds).

$D=-38t+220$

**What is Rachel's speed?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters per second
hints: Rachel's speed is the absolute value of the relationship's rate of change, which in linear relationships is represented by the slope of the graph.

The equation for $D$ is in *slope-intercept form*. This means that the slope of the graph is $-38$.

A slope of $-38$ means that Rachel's distance from the safe zone decreases by $38$ meters each second.
Rachel's speed is $38$ meters per second.
